name: av1_decoder_decoding_dataset
license_note: Original instructional/derivative content for training; avoid verbatim spec text.
schema: jsonl-like in JSON array; each item has fields: kind ∈ {instruction,worked_example,unit_qa}, instruction,input,output, and optional tags.
source_focus: AV1 decoding only (syntax parsing + reconstruction pipeline). No encoding guidance.

items.instruction: List the AV1 decoder pipeline stages end-to-end.
items.output: Parse OBUs → frame header parse → reference frame setup → tile group parsing → entropy decode of symbols → inverse quant/transform → intra/inter prediction (subpel/warp) → compound/OBMC → deblocking → CDEF → loop restoration (Wiener/SGR) → super-res (if any) → film grain (optional) → output/reorder.

Sequence header selects 64×64 or 128×128 SB. Partitions drive entropy contexts, transform bounds, edge availability, and loop-filter boundaries; decoder walks the partition tree to allocate mode info and neighbors.

DC, Smooth/Planar, Directional (angle set), Paeth, Filter‑Intra. Directional uses filtered references along quantized angles; Smooth fits gradient; DC averages; Paeth uses linear predictor with occlusion check; Filter‑Intra applies template filters.
Blocks pick single/compound refs from DPB slots (LAST..ALT2). Motion vectors at 1/4‑pel luma with 8‑tap luma and 4‑tap chroma filters; optional warped/global motion per ref.
tmp[x,y]=∑_{k=-3..4}H[k]*src[x+k,y]; dst[x,y]=clip((∑_{k=-3..4}V[k]*tmp[x,y+k]+round)>>shift). Coeff tables chosen by fractional offsets.
Blend current inter predictor with neighbors using overlapped windows to reduce seams at block borders.
Header signals per‑ref model (identity/translation/affine/projective). For blocks opting in, map (x,y) through model and sample with subpel filters; clip coordinates.
2D transforms = row×col from {DCT, ADST, FLIPADST, IDTX}. Inter up to 64×64, intra typically ≤32×32; asymmetrics allowed. ITX selected per block/tx set.
level[i]=qcoeff[i]*dequant(dc/ac)>>dq_shift; inverse scan to (x,y); apply inverse 1D rows then cols per tx_type; round/shift and clamp to bit depth.
Context‑adaptive multi‑symbol range/boolean coding; probs reset/updated per frame/tile as allowed. Symbols include partition, modes, coeffs, MVs, etc.
Base LF levels + ref/mode deltas + segmentation. HEV/flat decisions select tap patterns. Process vertical then horizontal per 4‑px edges; clamp outputs.
After deblock, before LR. Detect dominant direction per 8×8; apply constrained primary/secondary taps to suppress ringing while preserving edges.
Per plane per restoration unit: Wiener (separable) or SGR‑Proj (guided). Apply after CDEF with stripe overlap; clamp to bit depth.
If enabled, coded width < render width; upsample horizontally after LR and before film grain/output.
Use N‑bit modular order hints; reject refs where diff_mod(N, ref, cur) < 0 to avoid future references.

items.instruction: Top‑level OBU types relevant to decoding.
items.output: sequence_header, temporal_delimiter, frame_header (or frame), tile_group, metadata (e.g., film grain), padding.
(optional) temporal_delimiter → maybe new sequence_header → frame_header. If show_existing_frame: output and stop. Else parse all tile_group OBUs → reconstruct → run in‑loop tools.

frame_type/show flags → frame_id (if enabled) → frame_size/superres → render_size → reference mapping (primary_ref_frame, refresh flags) → screen/integer MV flags → order_hint → interp/tx settings → quant + deltas → segmentation map/params → delta_q/delta_lf flags → loop_filter → CDEF → LR → tile info → global motion → film grain src → coded_lossless derivation.
KEY resets refs; INTRA_ONLY is intra without full reset; INTER uses refs; SWITCH used for structure changes. show_frame displays immediately; show_existing_frame reuses DPB entry and skips tiles.

If frame_size_with_refs copies size from a ref: use it. Else if frame_size_override_flag: read explicit width/height; otherwise take from sequence header. Super‑res may reduce coded width; render size may differ.
Read coded denominator/scale; reconstruction uses coded_width; horizontal upscale later to render/output width.
refresh_frame_flags bitmask selects DPB slots to overwrite with the reconstructed output and order hint at end of frame.
Seeds probability contexts/segment defaults from a chosen reference unless error_resilient or unavailable, in which case defaults are used.

items.instruction: Order hint legality check at header time.
items.output: For each listed ref index: ensure diff_mod(bits, ref_oh, cur_oh) ≥ 0; otherwise stream error.

items.instruction: Interpolation and motion mode enable flags.
items.output: interpolation_filter can be switchable or fixed; header enables/disables OBMC/warp; force_integer_mv forbids fractional sampling for screen content.
tx_mode selects per‑block transform signaling vs derived; reduced_tx_set limits transform types to a subset for complexity savings.

items.instruction: Quant base and deltas; delta_q presence.
items.output: Read base_qindex; chroma DC/AC deltas if present. If delta_q_present=1, later per‑region delta_q adjusts local qindex at block roots.
segmentation_enabled toggles; segmentation_update_map and segmentation_temporal_update control map coding; feature data per segment follows when enabled.
If delta_lf_present=1, read resolution and whether deltas are per‑direction/per‑plane; later parsed at block roots.
loop_filter_level[0..3], sharpness; optional ref/mode delta arrays when enabled.
cdef_damping, cdef_bits (table size), and that many (luma,chroma) strength entries.
Per plane: lr_type (none/wiener/SGR/switchable) and RU size code; per‑frame limits for coeffs/eps; per‑RU params arrive later.
Uniform tiling uses tile_cols_log2/tile_rows_log2; non‑uniform provides explicit splits. Header may carry context_update_tile_id and per‑tile packet sizes (bytes).
Per reference: model type and fixed‑point parameters within range; store matrices for warped mode blocks.
Film grain parameters may be in frame header or metadata OBU; decoder uses one source and ensures grain is not referenced.

items.instruction: Worked: order hint legality check with 3‑bit hints.
items.output: diff_mod(3,6,5)=+1 OK; diff_mod(3,1,5)= (1-5)&7=4; 4 has msb=0 → +4 OK; diff_mod(3,3,5)=(3-5)&7=5; 5 has msb=1 → 5-8=−3 <0 → illegal (future). Drop ALT.

items.instruction: Worked: frame size derived from reference vs explicit.
items.input: frame_size_with_refs=1, use_ref=LAST with size 1920×1080; enable_superres=1, coded_denom=8 (scale=7/8).
items.output: Coded frame size= (1920*7/8)×1080 = 1680×1080 (integer per spec rounding). Render size remains 1920×1080 unless overridden.

items.input: base_q=128; delta_q_present=1; delta_q_res=2 (8×8). Region roots read dq:{+8, 0, −16}.
items.output: Region A q=136; Region B q=128; Region C q=112. Child blocks inherit parent q unless they start a new region root.

items.instruction: Worked: loop filter level composition on an inter edge.
items.input: lf_y=32; seg_delta=−4 (segment 3); ref_delta[ref=BWD]=+2; mode_delta[INTRA]=0; edge is inter with BWD and NEARMV.
items.output: level = 32 + (−4) + 2 + mode_delta[inter_class_of_NEARMV]. If inter_class delta=−1 → level=29. Clamp to [0..63].
out=(32*A + 32*B + 32)>>6 = ((A+B)+1)>>1 (rounded average).
fx=(3&3)*2 = 6 in 1/8‑pel index; fy=((−5)&3)*2 = 2 (after modular handling). Use 8‑tap tables at frac 6 (x) and 2 (y).
shift=max(0, D−⌊log2(S)⌋)=3−3=0; m=max(0, 20−(20>>0))=0 → res=0; high diff fully constrained at this S/D.

items.input: Horizontal taps h=[a,b,c,d,e,g] with center fixed and symmetry (a=e, b=d). Sum must equal 1<<shift.
items.output: Verify a+b+center+b+a == 1<<shift (gain=1). If sum≠target, reject/clip per spec; decoder enforces before convolution.

items.instruction: Worked: LR unit grid from code.
items.output: Mi=4×4 units → frame in Mi: 480×270. RU in Mi: 16×16. Grid: ceil(480/16)=30 cols, ceil(270/16)=17 rows.
Cols=4, rows=2. SB grid: 15×9 (ceil). Column SB widths ~ ceil(15/4) with equalization; decoder computes exact column/row boundaries per spec rounding rules.

items.input: QIndex→dequant: Y: dc=0 ac=0; U: dc=0 ac=0; V: dc=2 ac=0; monochrome=0.
items.output: plane_lossless[Y]=1, [U]=1, [V]=0 → coded_lossless=0. If monochrome=1, only Y matters → coded_lossless=1.

items.input: segmentation_enabled=1; segment 2 has QDELTA enabled with −12; base_q=144.
items.output: For blocks with seg_id=2, header‑scope qindex starts at clamp(144−12,0,MAXQ)=132 before any delta_q.

items.input: frame_id_length=15; delta_frame_id_length=7; prev_id=30000; current id read=200; ref id delta must be in (1..2^7).
items.output: diff=(200−30000) mod 2^15 = 200−(30000−32768)=1968; 1968 exceeds max delta (127) → error if used for ref matching.
Decoder reconstructs 1280×720; post‑filters run at coded size; upscaling to 1920×1080 occurs at presentation (and/or via super‑res if signaled).
Eligible neighbors: above only (inter). Blend window along top edge applied; left excluded because not inter.
Scale to chroma grid → typically round to nearest 1/2 or 1/4 pel depending on chroma filter; offsets halved vs luma; sample with 4‑tap chroma filter; clamp at borders.

items.input: TX 8×8 with EOB at position 12 per scan.
items.output: Decoder stops coefficient parsing after index 12; ITX only processes up to filled coefficients, improving speed.

items.instruction: Worked: tile packet size bounds check.
items.input: tile_size_bytes says 12000; frame OBU payload remaining=11000.
items.output: Invalid: tile size exceeds payload bounds → stream error; decoder aborts frame.
There exists one past (96 or 80) and one future (110) within window; compound allowed → skip_mode_present=1.

items.input: Adjacent RUs produce overlapping rows 4 px tall.
items.output: Decoder blends overlap with deterministic weights (e.g., top RU dominates top 2 rows, bottom RU the lower 2), preventing seams.

items.instruction: Worked: global motion projective safe division.
items.output: Compute denom in 64‑bit; if |denom| < epsilon (e.g., 1<<8) → reject model or clamp to identity to avoid overflow.

items.input: palette_size=4; entries={10,50,100,200}; index map for 4×4 given.
items.output: Decoder fills block by table lookup per index; no prediction filtering; apply bit‑depth clamp after write‑back.

items.input: bits=4; cur=9; ref=2. Return signed diff.

items.output: coded_width = floor(2560*7/8)=2240.

items.input: Assume tables yield dc=0, ac=0 at QIndex=0. Is plane lossless?
items.output: Yes for that plane; coded_lossless requires all active planes lossless.
cols=8; widths are nearly equal: either 4 or 5 SBs per column, summing to 33 as per spec equalization.

items.input: Block boundary is intra vs inter? tx size large vs small?
items.output: Intra edges often use stronger thresholds; if tx size is large or skip set, eligibility/strength adjust per masks; if base level=0 → no filter.

items.input: Sampling at x=−2 on 4:2:0 chroma plane width 640.
items.output: Clamp x to 0; use edge extension value for taps beyond border.
Opposing weight is 16; check (w+(64−w))=64. Use out=(w*A+(64−w)*B+32)>>6.

items.input: TX 16×16; EOB group indicates last coefficient < 64.
items.output: Decoder only parses up to index 63 in scan order; rest zeros.

items.input: How many discrete directions are evaluated for 8×8?
items.output: A small finite set (e.g., 8 directions). Decoder picks max energy direction from this set.
Mi grid 480×270; RU Mi 16×16 → 30×17 units.

items.input: If denom negative after fixed‑point calc, what then?
items.output: Signed division still valid; ensure no underflow and clip coords; reject if magnitude too small per spec.
skip: no coeffs parsed → predictor only. txfm_skip: coeffs parsed but apply identity transform.

items.input: monochrome=1; how to parse chroma params?
items.output: Chroma quant/LF/CDEF/LR fields omitted or defaulted; only luma processed.

items.input: Current coded width 1280; ref coded width 960.
items.output: Horizontal scale factor maps current x to ref x; use scaled strides and tap tables based on ref scale.

items.instruction: OBMC window length on a 16×16 block.
items.output: A small border strip (e.g., 2–4 px) with pre‑tabulated weights along the shared edge.
Decoder later reads separate deltas per vertical/horizontal (and possibly per plane) at block roots aligned to delta_lf_res.
Ignore primary_ref_frame seeding; initialize contexts from defaults.
Only subset of transform types legal; decoder enforces at block parse and omits disallowed symbols.

items.input: Can film‑grain frames be used as refs?
items.output: No. Grain is applied at output and not stored for inter prediction.
No. Correct order is deblock → CDEF → LR → (grain).
